codeReview.summary: The implementation replaces Ink runtime usage with `@clack/prompts` and covers VAL-PROMPTS-001..006: single-select (`p.select`) and multi-select (`p.multiselect`) flows are present, cancellation is handled consistently via `p.isCancel` + `handleCancel`, install side effects are deferred until after prompt completion, keypress-bleed-prone transition state was removed in favor of sequential async prompts, and CLI now imports `interactive.ts` backed by `@clack/prompts`. Handoff interactive checks corroborate 001/002/003/005 and git-status checks corroborate 004.

issues.description: Non-interactive guarding checks only `process.stdin.isTTY`. If stdin is TTY but stdout/stderr are redirected, prompt rendering can still initialize and emit control sequences to non-TTY output. Consider guarding on output TTY as well to avoid non-TTY compatibility leaks.

Cancellation consistency is implemented for prompt stages, but there is no explicit SIGINT handling once install starts. Ctrl+C during `runInstall(...)` can terminate mid-write with behavior different from the prompt-stage `Operation cancelled.` path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `cli-refactor-worker` mandates strict tests-first TDD, but this feature’s own handoff feedback indicates UI-migration work needed implementation-first before stable tests. The skill should explicitly allow this exception path for prompt-engine migrations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/cli-refactor-worker/SKILL.md:21 requires tests-first; handoff 2026-03-03T16-23-13-500Z...ac254eeb...json:130-131 suggests updating the skill for UI migrations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Manual TTY validation depended on `tuistory` plus `node-pty` installation steps that are not captured in shared testing knowledge, causing avoidable setup/debug time during prompt verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl (session ac254eeb...) shows `which tuistory || ...` and `npm install -g node-pty`; .factory/library/user-testing.md has no `tuistory` guidance (no matches for 'tuistory').

summary: Reviewed feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `5a40732` diff for prompt runtime paths. VAL-PROMPTS-001..006 are functionally satisfied with two non-blocking risks noted (partial TTY guard and install-phase SIGINT consistency). Report saved for scrutiny synthesis.
